title: AuditLedgerEntry
description: One record in the append-only, hash-chained medicolegal audit ledger (the registered internal component `medicolegal-audit-ledger`). Persisted one-per-line to .heydoc-data/audit-ledger.jsonl by both report writers (verification/run.js, integration/trunk-pipeline.js). CRITICAL DATA RULE: this record carries NO patient-identifiable information — no facts, no demographics, no candidate output text. It holds only audit anchors: the SHA-256 candidate_output_hash, run/trunk/session metadata, the pass gate, per-check results, and receipt METADATA (request_id/upstream/mode/timestamp only — never payloads). The exact output text lives in the separately-governed, synthetic-only content store, content-addressed by candidate_output_hash. Tamper-evidence: entry_hash chains over this record's canonical content plus prev_hash, so any insertion, edit, or reorder breaks the chain (verifyChain()).

seq.description: Monotonic 0-based sequence number of this entry in the ledger. seq=0 is the genesis-linked first entry.

entry_id.description: Unique identifier for this ledger entry. Convention: 'ledger-<epoch-ms>-<random-7-chars>'.

recorded_at_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this entry was appended to the ledger. Distinct from the verification run timestamp; this is the audit-write time.

prev_hash.description: entry_hash of the immediately preceding ledger entry, forming the append-only chain. The first entry (seq=0) uses the fixed genesis value sha256:0000…0000 (64 zeros).

entry_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of this entry's content (all fields except entry_hash) concatenated with prev_hash. This is the chain link: recomputing it for every entry and checking prev_hash linkage detects any tampering, insertion, or reorder.

run_id.description: The pipeline run_id this audit entry records. Joins to the VerificationReport.run_id and the per-run artifacts.

trunk_id.description: Optional. The trunk whose output was verified, when known (present for runTrunkWithGrounding, absent for the standalone run.js).

session_ref.description: Optional. Encounter-scoped pseudonymous session reference — NEVER demographics or IHI. Enables joining all audit entries for one consultation without storing identity.

candidate_output_hash.description: The medicolegal anchor: SHA-256 of the exact trunk output (from verify()). Also the content-store key — when content_persisted=true the output text is at .heydoc-data/content/<hex>.txt.

pass.description: The VerificationReport pass gate for this run.

check_results.description: Per-check pass/fail summary mirrored from the VerificationReport. No reasons/excerpts that could carry output content — passed booleans only.

receipts.description: Receipt METADATA only — the proof that retrieval happened, never the retrieved content. Lets any past decision's receipts be retrieved by request_id.

mode.description: Effective mode of the run that produced this entry. mock/staging entries are synthetic; live entries are real and must never have content_persisted=true until session-bound persistence + consent are enforced.

content_persisted.description: Whether the exact output text was written to the synthetic-only content store for this entry. MUST be false for any non-synthetic (live) output — the patient-data-minimisation guard. true is only valid for synthetic/mock+staging data.
